data.title: Tilray Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 in-line, revenue of $209.5M beats by $3.75M
data.description:
• Tilray press release (NASDAQ:TLRY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 in-line.
  • Revenue of $209.5M (+4.7% Y/Y) beats by $3.75M.
  • Balance Sheet Strengthened to $265 Million in Cash; Net Debt Reduced to $4 Million
  • Reiterates Fiscal Year 2026 Adjusted EBITDA Outlook of $62 Million – $72 Million

data.title: Neogen rises after reporting Q1 results
data.description:
• Neogen press release (NASDAQ:NEOG): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line.
  • Revenue of $209.2M (-3.6% Y/Y) beats by $5.05M.
  • The Company is reaffirming its full-year outlook for fiscal year 2026. Revenue is anticipated to be in the range of $820 million to $840 million and Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be in the range of $165 million to $175 million. Capital expenditures are expected to total approximately $50 million.

data.title: Delta Air Lines in charts: Q3 TRASM stable Y/Y, CASM nudges higher, fuel expense dips
Autoliv and China's HSAE to form JV for advanced automotive safety electronics
Helen of Troy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.06, revenue of $431.8M beats by $14.1M
data.description:
• Helen of Troy press release (NASDAQ:HELE): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $431.8M (-8.9% Y/Y) beats by $14.1M.
  • Provides Fiscal 2026 Outlook:Consolidated Net Sales of $1.739-$1.780 Billion vs $1.75B consensusGAAP Diluted Loss Per Share of $29.90-$29.40Adjusted Diluted EPS of $3.75-$4.25 vs $4.58 consensus
  • The Company expects fiscal 2026 GAAP diluted loss per share of $29.90 to $29.40
  • The Company expects third quarter fiscal 2026 GAAP diluted EPS of $1.85 to $2.05 and non-GAAP adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $1.55 to $1.80 vs $1.98 consensus, which implies an adjusted diluted EPS decline of 41.9% to 32.6%, compared to the third quarter of fiscal 2025.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Delta Air Lines delivers strong Q4 outlook despite missing Q3 revenue
Hycroft Mining proposes $100M stock offering
data.description:
• Hycroft Mining (NASDAQ:HYMC) has proposed a public offering of Class A shares to raise gross proceeds of around $100 million.
  • Net proceeds from the offering will be used to expand and accelerate exploration and drilling programs, supporting the company's strategy to unlock additional near-mine and district-scale potential at the Hycroft Mine. Net proceeds are also expected to be used to address general corporate and working capital needs.
  • HYMC --2.23% premarket to $6.95
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ferrari slides as investors weigh long-term guidance, new EV plans
data.description:
Ferrari N.V. (NYSE:RACE) updated its long-term financial targets as part of its Capital Markets Day presentation on Thursday. The Italian automaker said it is aiming for strong growth and profitability through 2030. 

In terms of guidance, the company expects net revenue to reach approximately €9.0 billion by 2030, representing a compound annual growth rate of around 5%, primarily driven by high-value sports cars, customized models, and other car-related activities sustained by a robust order book. Ferrari (NYSE:RACE) noted that its expanded product mix and increased personalization are expected to support the projected revenue growth.

Profitability targets for Ferrari (RACE) include achieving EBIT of at least €2.75 billion with a minimum margin of 30% in 2030, supported by the premium and limited-edition product lineup and enhanced personalization options. EBITDA is targeted at €3.6 billion or higher, with an EBITDA margin of at least 40%. Ferrari (RACE) noted that the targets reflect higher industrial costs, R&D, and marketing expenses to develop new products and expand the brand, which are anticipated to be offset by greater free cash flow generation.

Ferrari (RACE) forecasts cumulative industrial free cash flow of about €8.0 billion over 2026–2030, after accounting for capital expenditures of approximately €4.7 billion and other operating changes. The company noted that it has already surpassed its 2026 profitability milestones ahead of schedule and aims for cash conversion rates above 50%.

On the production front, Ferrari (RACE) sees an average of four new car launches per year planned between 2026 and 2030. In 2030, the product lineup will be 40% ICE, 40% hybrid, and 20% electric. The percent of electric vehicles was lowered from a prior target of 40% of the portfolio,

"With the new Ferrari Elettrica, we once again affirm our will to progress by uniting the discipline of technology, the creativity of design and the craft of manufacturing," stated Ferrari Executive Chairman John Elkann.

Shares of Ferrari (RACE) fell 12.6% in premarket action on Thursday.
